name: Todd Russell
title: Mechanical Engineer
tagline: Mechatronics · Automation · Precision Manufacturing · Solar Technology
summary: Engineer who fuses a passion for mechatronics and design with a drive to push the boundaries of what is possible. Thrive solving complex problems in novel ways.

highlights: Drove 34% improvement in initial module efficiency and 35× improvement in module degradation lifespan as lead of module development.
Achieved world-record 24% perovskite module efficiency through joint development with NREL.
Increased laser scribing throughput by 500%, yield by 5%, and reduced positional repeatability from 60μm to 20μm through automation and machine vision integration.
Reduced total positional variation throughout the production line by 70% via targeted mechanical and process improvements.
Spearheaded company-wide defect tracking initiative — identified two defects present on 50% of modules causing 20× degradation impact.
Developed user-friendly prototype to collect patient data and evaluate a groundbreaking non-invasive method of measuring intracranial pressure via ultrasound in patients with acute neurologic injury.
Designed and modeled bespoke soundproofing, heat recovery, and controlled climate enclosure systems in SolidWorks.
Built master assemblies composed of thousands of components nested within subassemblies.
Improved the company's pack-and-go subassembly system to streamline workflow.

experience.company: National Security Innovation Network, U.S. Air Force
highlights: Lead author of cost-benefit analysis on stationing aerospace ground equipment at Gila Bend Airfield to prepare diverted F-35 aircraft for return to Luke Air Force Base.
Recommended strategy was implemented at Luke AFB with planned nationwide rollout.

experience.company: Mechanical Engineering Competition Team at Berkeley
highlights: Designed, proposed, and manufactured an EVA tool attachment mechanism for astronaut utility belts for NASA's Micro-g NExT Challenge.
Editor-in-Chief of technical report utilizing Compliance Matrix, FEA, FMEA Risk Matrix, and Trade Study Pugh Chart.
Selected for Phase 2 — design was manufactured, tested, and evaluated at NASA's Neutral Buoyancy Lab.

education.degree: M.S. Mechanical Engineering · Certificate of Engineering Leadership
education.details: Gordon Fellow, Gordon Institute of Engineering Leadership
GPA 3.89 / 4.0 · Cum Laude (Top 10%) · Dean's List, College of Engineering
Control of UAVs · Mechatronics Design · Manufacturing & Tolerancing · Control Systems Engineering
